cards.front: What is a class in Object Oriented Programming (OOP) in Java?
cards.back: A blueprint or template that defines the properties and behaviors of an object

cards.front: How do you declare a class in Java?
cards.back: Using the `class` keyword followed by the name of the class

cards.front: What is the purpose of a constructor in a Java class?
cards.back: To initialize objects when they are created

cards.front: What is the difference between a class and an object?
cards.back: A class is a blueprint, while an object is an instance of the class

cards.front: How do you assign an object reference variable to an object?
cards.back: Using the `=` operator, e.g., `Object obj = new MyClass();`
